title: Diploknema grandiflora
intro: Diploknema grandiflora, also known as the 'Indian cherry' or 'wild cherry', is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Diploknema grandiflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Diploknema grandiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia. This plant belongs to the genus Diploknema, which is part of the Sapindaceae family. Diploknema grandiflora is closely related to other plants in the Sapindaceae family, such as the soapberry trees. The classification of Diploknema grandiflora is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, family - Sapindaceae, genus - Diploknema.

Further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use.
